name: Paracetamol
uses: Pain relief and fever reduction for mild to moderate pain (headache, muscle ache, toothache).
dosage: Adults: commonly 500–1000 mg every 4–6 hours as needed; do not exceed maximum daily dose on product label. Adjust for children by weight per pediatric labeling.
sideEffects: Generally well tolerated; rare rash, liver injury with overdose or chronic excess.
precautions: Avoid or use cautiously with liver disease; avoid combining multiple products containing paracetamol; do not exceed daily limits.

name: Ibuprofen
uses: Pain, inflammation, and fever (e.g. sprains, menstrual pain, headache).
dosage: Adults: often 200–400 mg every 4–6 hours with food as labeled; use lowest effective dose for shortest time.
sideEffects: Stomach upset, increased bleeding risk; kidney effects with prolonged use or dehydration.
precautions: Avoid with active ulcers, kidney disease, certain heart conditions, third trimester pregnancy; take with food.

name: Aspirin
uses: Pain, fever, anti-inflammatory; in some cases cardiovascular prevention only under medical supervision.
dosage: Use only as directed on label or by prescriber; low-dose cardiac use is different from analgesic dosing.
sideEffects: GI bleeding risk, bruising; Reye's syndrome risk in children with viral illness.
precautions: Not for children/teens with viral illness without clinician advice; caution with bleeding disorders.

name: Amoxicillin
uses: Bacterial infections (e.g. some respiratory, ear, urinary infections) when prescribed by a clinician.
dosage: Prescription-only; dose depends on infection type, age, and kidney function—follow prescriber exactly.
sideEffects: Diarrhea, nausea, rash; rare serious allergic reactions.
precautions: Penicillin allergy; complete prescribed course; discuss with clinician if prior antibiotic reactions.

name: Azithromycin
uses: Certain bacterial respiratory and skin infections per prescription.
dosage: Prescription-only; dosing schedules vary (e.g. multi-day packs)—use as directed.
sideEffects: GI upset, diarrhea; QT prolongation risk in susceptible patients.
precautions: Drug interactions (e.g. some antiarrhythmics); liver issues—use under medical supervision.

name: Omeprazole
uses: Reduces stomach acid; used for GERD, gastric protection in some cases as directed.
dosage: Typically once daily before a meal; OTC vs prescription strengths differ—follow label or prescriber.
sideEffects: Headache, diarrhea, B12/magnesium issues with long-term use.
precautions: Long-term PPI use should be reviewed by a clinician; interactions with certain drugs (e.g. clopidogrel—seek advice).

name: Cetirizine
uses: Allergic rhinitis, hives—reduces itching and sneezing.
dosage: Adults: commonly 10 mg once daily; may be reduced for kidney impairment—read label.
sideEffects: Drowsiness in some individuals, dry mouth.
precautions: Use caution when driving until you know your response; pregnancy/breastfeeding—consult clinician.

name: Loratadine
uses: Seasonal allergies, allergic rhinitis, some skin allergies.
dosage: Typically 10 mg once daily for adults; follow pediatric dosing on label.
precautions: Liver impairment may require dose adjustment; check interactions.

name: Metformin
uses: Type 2 diabetes blood sugar management as prescribed.
dosage: Prescription-only; titrated by clinician; often taken with meals.
sideEffects: GI upset, rare lactic acidosis; B12 deficiency with long-term use.
precautions: Kidney function monitoring; hold around contrast procedures per clinician; avoid excess alcohol.

name: Atorvastatin
uses: Lowers LDL cholesterol; cardiovascular risk reduction when prescribed.
dosage: Prescription-only; taken once daily, often evening.
sideEffects: Muscle aches, liver enzyme changes; rare muscle injury.
precautions: Report unexplained muscle pain; grapefruit interaction for some statins—follow prescriber advice.

name: Levothyroxine
dosage: Highly individualized; take consistently on empty stomach as directed.
precautions: Many drug interactions; separate from calcium/iron supplements—follow pharmacist guidance.

name: Salbutamol
uses: Relief of bronchospasm in asthma/COPD via inhaler as prescribed.
dosage: As prescribed; rescue inhaler frequency should be reviewed if used often.
precautions: Seek urgent care if rescue inhaler does not relieve symptoms or breathing worsens.
